title: atriplex hortensis proles microtheca
intro: Atriplex hortensis proles microtheca, also known as the microleaved orach, is a plant species belonging to the amaranth family (Amaranthaceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is rich in vitamins A and C, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The microleaved orach has been valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the microleaved orach include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
